title: Meh, and not really suitable for small wrists
text:
I bought this watch to go along with the Dexcom G6 app on my phone (this is a continuous glucose monitoring app). This app is only compatible with watches running Wear OS, so the Samsung watches were off the table when considering my options. I have an android phone, so Apple watches were also off the table. The Dexcom G6 app works as it is supposed to on this watch for anyone interested, although this will be a small percentage reading this review. I've had this for several months so here's my take on it. . .

First the good/neutral:
Cost -- this watch has a LOT of features compared to other watches on the market for its price.
Battery -- I have been getting 2 days out of it before needing to charge it.
Heart rate monitor -- I'm not training for a marathon with it or anything, but it seems to roughly capture what zone I should be in when working out (which is all I'm looking for) -- it's uncomfortable though so I really don't wear it much while working out
Step Counter -- seems to match reasonably well with my phone
Maneuverability -- The initial version that comes loaded on it is a little clunky, but the first update fixes a lot of the issues so it's easier to get around in the menus
Phone/messages -- viewing messages/phone calls is fine. It's tough to type a message on it as you might expect (I've not found much reason to, but I did try it out), and I did make one brief phone call with it and it was fine.

Then the not-so-good:
Aesthetics -- it's clunky and ugly and only comes is glaring plastic colors (black, white, or neon yellow/green)
Size -- it is not meant for a woman's wrist. It is way too big and thick and is very uncomfortable as a result.
Comfort -- The watch band that comes with it is hot and sweaty. I've changed it out for a leather one, but it's still very thick and uncomfortable. The back of the watch has the charging port/sensors and is not smooth, so it leaves indents in your skin, which is also uncomfortable. It hits my wrist bone and there's really no way to place it on my arm so that it's comfortable because it is just too big. Because it's so thick, it also is uncomfortable to wear with shirts that have elastic cuffs (coats, gloves, sweatshirts, and in my case my lab coat at work)
Charger -- it doesn't stay connected to the charger very well and the cord that comes with it is WAY too short to be useful.
Setup -- once it's connected to your phone it works seamlessly, but the initial set up was a PITA. It did not want to recognize the connection and both watch and phone had to be restarted and the bluetooth turned off and on multiple times before they would recognize each other.

Takehome message: Meh. It's OK, and I'll sort of use it, but it's not really something I'd run out and get again or recommend to someone. There needs to be a lot of work done to make smart watches more suited for smaller wrists because the comfort and aesthetics is really where this thing falls majorly flat, but the only watches out there geared toward smaller wrists cost a ton of money and hardly have any features to go with that high price tag.

title: I am truly disappointed with Samsung
text: I am truly disappointed with Samsung!  i loved my Samsung Gear fit watch.  not to Bulky,  tracked my steps,  linked well with my phone.  partial text message, I ran a 5k and sweat as expected.  and the little piece of gas that covers the pulse sensor came off on my skin as I was running.  Thank God I noticed it and realized where it was supposed to go.  I put it back in place and continued the run.  within a few days watch started to act funny.  it would not turn off at times and at other times would not turn on.  TThe touch screen then stopped wotking.  I could see all the messages and the tracking coming thru to the phone but I couldn't  use the touch screen.  I sent the phone into Samsung and they refused to fix it stating that the warranty was void due to water Damage.  I have never gotten the watch wet by any other means besides sweating.  How does a watch that can supposedly be worn in the shower, not stand up tho sweating.  So........hte watch was sent back and I then contacted amazon to see about  using the warrant on the product that I purchased through Amazon.  I was told tha tI could not use the Amazon warranty until after the Samsung warranty had expired.  So ...about 1 week after I got the watch back,  I plugged it in and it began to work again.  it lasted for about 3 weeks then started to make a loud / super loud whining scream . It then overheat because I could not cut it off .  So sit and wait for 1 warranty to run out so that I can use the other.

title: Horrible Bluetooth
text: I got these this morning, charged the  up, connected to my phone and download the software update. Then I disconnected from my phone and paired with my Galaxy Watch 46mm LTE and set out for a walk. The moment I went outside, the audio started cutting in and out. The only way the audio stays clear is if I hold the watch under my chin. (This does not happen with the same watch and my older pair of airpods) The moment I drop my hand, it's like someone is trying to communicate to me frantically in muted Morse Code. Buyers remorse like a mf at the moment. I called Samsung for help and they told me to try and clean them. They are hours old. Then I was referred back to Amazon. I've looked everywhere online, and this seems to be a big problem with no fix.

title: Fastest Turn-around in the West!
text:
PREFACE: I purchased the Bragi Dash as a kickstarter and have been using them daily up until last week. A firmware update caused mine to have an issue and Bragi is processing my replacement. I purchased this unit because going from mono to stereo back to mono sucks. I wanted try out a different model than what i was used to for comparison's sake.

Ok. Here we go. They arrived this afternoon, 6 hours ago. 2 hours later, the indicator lights on the charging case were green, stating they were fully charged. I downloaded the Samsung Gear App and synched everything up. Initially, I was impressed at how everything came together nicely. I assumed they would. I'm on a GalaxyS6Edge and they're also a Samsung product. Its one of the reasons i chose this model over others. I went through the settings, turned off all the notifications as i didn't want interruptions while listening to my audiobook. I adjusted the unit volume and verified batteries were full in the app.
1 hour later:
* the app showed the batteries un-evenly down to half on the left and 2/5 on the right (right was the primary sync with the phone so i assumed it would go faster.
* the left earbud was constantly sending out notifications and disconnecting. The fit was actually decent so i didn't feel the need to swap out the gels. I'd removed them and re-inserted as the instructions suggested to re-sync and that would last a minute or two before the notifications/beeping would start again. I unpaired and re-paired, no change.
* on 3 occasions, both buds lost connection with the phone, despite being within 2 ft of it.

Those are my functional complaints with the device. I also have concerns for people who decide to look into this model for themselves. I've already packed it up for a refund so it aids me none.
* The charging case does not magnetically hold the buds in place when lined for charging. When you close the lid over the buds, the top pushes the buds down against the charging buttons/pins and that's when it begins. If the hinge ever breaks on the case, you're done for. The case is also plastic, be ware.
* The fit and look were fine, but i never felt confident it would hold.
* Instructions say to avoid touching the heart rate monitor pad on the buds when inserting in your ear to prevent mis-readings when synched up for activities. Its a small personal grudge but i don't want to have to think about how i'm holding them to get them in, i just wanna pop the buds in and go.

Advice: Avoid these earbuds... at least until they work out the kinks, improve the battery life and get a better charging method/case. As much as i love my Bragi, they have their own batch of issues. I do feel despite their issues, i still like them better than these IconX earbuds.

title: excellent battery life.  Only downside is display is not bright
text: I switched to the Vivoactive HR watch from a Galaxy Sport and a Galaxy Watch 46mm.  Both Samsung watches had a day to 1.5 day battery life.  The Vivoactive is giving me a weeks worth of battery life and still isn't completely drained.  That is not using the GPS function of either watch.  When using the GPS functions all three watches will drop faster but the Samsungs at a much faster rate than the Garmin.  The screen on the Vivoactive is not as bright as I would like it at times but if that is what is making the battery life so much better I can live with it without complaint.  This is definitely a 5 star watch to me

title: Not Worth the Money
text: I had high hopes for this device, but I was incredibly disappointed on a number of counts. First, the water protection has to be engaged, and once engaged, other features can't be accessed. I was out jogging in the rain and so I thought it would be wise to use this feature. It turned out to be inconvenient. Also, the navigation turned out to be inferior in terms of tracking distance. It was off by almost a full mile on a twelve-mile jog (I had done the jog twice already with this device as well as 100s of times with my Fitbit for some reason, it was off on the third jog with it, which was the final straw for me). The notification functions for email and messages are no better than what my Fitbit Versa 2 offers, nor are the compatible apps. The only difference is that I can go without my phone and still have a "phone."  I can't use Audible, Amazon music, Pandora, Libby. I did my best to work with Spotify (Samsung music required specific formats), which meant programming in my music. Ok, did that. Their audiobook integration is clunky. And, that's the only audiobook option I seemed to have. I can do Spotify on my Versa; so, no gain there. My Versa can actually do Pandora, so it has that feature over the Samsung watch. The only really neat thing the Samsung can do is an upper body analysis. I don't use most of the watch features with my Fitbit since I need to have my phone with me anyway. The whole point of the Samsung was to be able to use it without the phone. On the phone side, I needed multiple apps to take advantage of different features. Could Samsung have done any worse with this? My Versa 2 is three years old now, still works, goes in the shower, the pool, etc., and was a fraction of the price. However, I really want to run without my phone. So, I've switched phones and providers to get the Apple Watch Ultra - it's proven to be worth the price.  I recommend against this particular watch given the price. It simply doesn't have the features/functionality to warrant that price tag.

title: OK little camera crap software support
text:
I would have given the camera 4 or 5 stars but I took 2 stars off because of Samsung. You must have a newer samsung phone to run this cameras app. Now the camera will function just fine with out the apps but you lose some functionality and some on the fly functionality with out the gear 360 applications which only run on samsung phones (newer ones). I have an edge Samsung that would not run the software ("This version is not compatible with your device") I do have a hacked version of the software that works well on that samsung but not so much on other devices. SO just know what your getting, a good camera that wants to force you into using only samsung gear with it.

As far as the camera is a bit odd to grab \ hold with out a handle \ tripod and you will smudge the lenses easily if your not careful. Other than that it works well and I have had little problem with it.

Pro:
Good pics
Good Video
360 shooting is nice
Compact design
nice features
Editing suite (MAC and windows) is pretty good and free with camera purchase.

Con:
Requires Samsung devices (or a hacked version of their app) to access on the fly features and settings
expensive for a device that is limited on what android devices it will communicate with.
battery life is a bit limited but not terrible considering its size.

title: Too big, slide out of ear (mic records when headset is off hence my 1 star update)
text:
Edit: today I tried to place a call and I couldn't hear the other line, then I noticed my phone said the sound was going to Bluetooth. I check my Samsung watch 4 which was the only one connected to Bluetooth. That wasn't it.

Then I decided to check my headphones next. Turns out they were secretly recording my voice, even thou the Bluetooth on the phone wasn't saying they were connected and all the lights on them were off indicating they weren't even on.

That is so creepy, I felt so violated. It's been almost a year since I had these and only now I figured out they've been listening all along even while off. No wonder they often have low battery when I try to actually use them after they sat in the box a few days.

Anker this is not right! You violated my privacy. I did not give you my consent.

-Great sound, Great connectivity, USB C is nice but the fitment is poor.
-They fit uncomfortable at best. Slide out at worst. Was walking and the vibrations made them slide out.
This morning on my second day with them i was having breakfast and they wouldn't stay in my ear longer than a minute because of jaw movement.
-Charging doc is nice and they do feel like quality.
I really wish they fit better as the sound is great. I'd hate to return them, but i sadly might have to. I hope they don't get sent to a landfill.
The price is great if these fit in your ears. I tried the other tips but they are terrible. Either too small or too big.

title: Very Dissappointed
text:
After reading numerous feedbacks and reviews on smartwatches, I decided on the Samsung Gear S2 classic. I was apprehensive about purchasing a refurbished watch, but I couldn't justify the cost of a new smartwatch. Since I had recently decided to try a refurbished power bank and when I received it, it looked and performed as new, I decided to try a Certified Refurbished smartwatch. I ordered the Certified Refurbished Samsung Gear S2 SM-R735T smartwatch. I was extremely happy to receive the watch until I opened the box. I was really disappointed to open the box and find the watch had been put into a zip lock bag and put into the box with a generic charging dock and charging cord with no extra packing to keep the items from banging around in the box during shipping. There was no extra short strap as shown in the amazon add and not one bit of information as to what I had ordered, not even a quick start guide. The only documentation in the box was a card saying it was Certified refurbished which didn't even say what it was. I might have expected a inexpensive 15.00-30.00 dollar watch to be shipped like that but not an expensive watch like a Samsung Gear smartwatch. I am really dissatisfied with the seller and not with the Samsung smartwatch.
I placed the watch on the charger and was surprised when it got so hot the watch shut down and showed a message that it had overheated and had to cool down. I don't know if it overheated because it wasn't a Samsung charging dock or because of the specific generic brand of charger. After it cooled down it charged the watch. I have charged the watch since and it gets warm but it hasn't overheated again. I don't know if it damaged the battery or the watch or affected the longevity when it overheated, but I was able to update the software and down load some of the apps OK.
The watch seems a bit larger and thicker than I expected, however it has a great display and it seems to have plenty of apps and watch faces and is very comfortable to wear. I feel the Samsung Gear S2 Classic is a great watch and I find the rotating bezel to be a great advantage in selecting and operating the various apps. The battery last about a day and a half with minimal to moderate use. I was able to connect the watch with my Samsung phone without any problems and was able to download most of the apps that were required. The one app that I couldn't get to work was the Buddy app. The voice app was a bit slow but it worked OK. I was able to make and receive phone calls through the watch and the voice was clear at both ends. The volume is a bit low on the watch but it works well. The health app covers all the basics that I need and is fairly easy to understand and use, I have not used the GPS and I am still unsure if the GPS is independent of the phone or not.
Overall I am satisfied I selected the Gear S2 Classic. It does everything I need and more. I feel that it's a gamble buying a Certified Refurbished item and it depends on the seller wheather you get what you pay for or not.

title: Absolute Trash
text:
I got this watch as a birthday gift (purchased through Amazon) and it was suppose to retire my old Samsung Gear S3. I had it for 3 weeks and hadn't experienced any issues until I took a shower one night after setting the water lock. After I dried the watch the screen was very slow to respond and by the next day the watch had stopped accepting input on the screen and was experiencing "ghost touches" and would burn through a full battery overnight. I sent the watch to Samsung for repair as it was inside of the warranty period obviously and got a reply back with a quote for repair for roughly $220. I was told there was water damage and this wasn't covered under the warranty. After explaining the situation and how the damage occurred and providing proof of purchase with less than a month of ownership they still denied my claim.

It's absolutely crazy a watch that claims to be waterproof to 5 ATM can't handle a simple shower and Samsung isn't willing to work with a customer. I've always been a Samsung buyer and owned multiple products of theirs but this put a bad taste in my mouth....Research the issue of water damage and you will see many complaints on forums of people experiencing the same issues and Samsung issues the same reply and charges for repair. Samsung isn't willing to admit there's an issue and leaves the customer on the hook for the repair.

title: A nice watch with an annoying flaw
text: In general the Galaxy Watch 3 is a nice watch.  There are two problems with my watch: 1) the floor/stair function is unreliable and does not work most of the time, 2) occasionally the sleep function does not automatically track the sleep period and sleep time must be entered manually.  The biggest negative with this watch is the poor implementation of the floor/step function.  Samsung has  had problems with this feature for years.  They can't get it right while other watch vendors do.  When I see long term unresolved problems  persist while the competition succeeds, I question how much Samsung cares about their product and the level of resource  they are willing to commit to fixing nagging problems.

title: Best Yet!
text:
I want to start off with what I didn't like with this device because its not a lot. Firstly is the bezel, its not the worst thing in the world but it is very noticeable and is worse than some of the competition or even previous ticwatches. The other issue is with using Google Assistant, on the phone usually when you ask google it will show a confirmation that it did what was asked but on the watch it will just close out and you are left wondering if the right thing was done or if anything was done. Usually everything has been correct but i wish they displayed like a checkmark or something to confirm what was done.

Moving on to the good stuff is everything else, #1 is the speed, this is by far the most responsive watch I've owned and finally something that I feel is comparable to apple watch in performance without the price of something the like galaxy gear devices. The screen is sharp and clear, and everything works as expected. At $200 it is hard to imagine anything that is this powerful. I've owned the fossil smartwatch, samsung smartwatch, and several Ticwatches, this is the second watch after the galaxy that I felt was actually helpful and not a hinderance and at its price I feel like it is worth as opposed to the galaxy watch which feels a little too much for what a smartwatch is used for. Battery life is incredible, the new processor in this device really works magic.

title: Very good value. Phone calls not great, no ambient sound options which is a bummer.
text:
Update update.
I got in touch with anker direct customer service. They offer an 18 month warranty on their products from defects. They were awesome to work with, and they communicated very well. They paid for shipping for me to send my broken earbuds back to them, and once they received them, they were very quick to send me a brand new pair no hassle. Their customer service is great.

Update. They quit working one day after the return window closed. The left earbud only flashes a red light.

I have tried a few different ear buds, and these by far are the best bang for the buck. They are my wife's, but I used them for a few days and they are great.

My first pair of ear buds were the samsung gear icon x 2018. They killed my ears, and the touch pads on the side were horrible. The zolo liberty ear buds are very comfortable for me for 2 to 3 hours of use. After that I experience ear fatigue. I have yet to find a set of ear buds that don't fatigue my ears. I think it's just part of the deal. These, however, for my ears are some of the most comfortable I have tried. Fitment is subjective. Everyone's ears are different.

As far as battery life goes, these are great. I get 3 hour of listening out of them before they need a charge. The case will charge the buds 3 or 4 times before it's dead. Also, you can store them in the case for quite a while. It took me over a week to drain them to the point that the case needed a charge.

As far as sound goes, these are great. They have very responsive and immerseful lows mids and highs. Compared to the Samsung icon x's, these sound better, in my opinion.

These do make Bluetooth phone calls. The listeners on the other end said the quality was ok. I didn't have any problem communicating or hearing anyone. However, these ear buds seal off your ear canal, so your own voice is heard through your bones, which makes talking weird. It's like talking with earplugs in. The sound only comes from the right earbud which is also weird. The Samsung's, as well as the jabra elite 65t have ambient sound turned on while using the phone, and the sound comes from both ear buds, which is much better. Calling on the zolo's works, but there are better options available for calls.

Overall, these are pretty comfortable, quality is great, battery life is great, value is great. Phone calls are not the strong point of these.

title: Superb form factor, horrible Bluetooth connectivity
text:
I really wanted to keep these - I even bought three! - was REALLY hoping that my Bluetooth connectivity issues are because a lemon, but no - there is something not right with this model.
I returned already two and will return tomorrow the third one. So sad.

Why do I love these?
The form factor wins thee day for me - the idea of putting a bean in your ear - is just brilliant. These are the most comfortable buds I ever had (and I tried quite a few) - even in the most intense workout they just keep in my ears. They don't "stick out" in any way. Love it!

Why do I return them?
The Bluetooth connectivity is just horrible. And I am a Samsung person - Samsung phone, Samsung tablet, Samsung watch - so I thought what can get wrong with Samsung buds? I made sure I am on the latest firmware update on every buds set.
Apparently I was wrong: echoing between the buds, delays between the buds (one bud is a bit delayed after the other), losing connectivity to the buds, losing connectivity to one bud - all at random.
Even if I place the phone in my pants pocket - connectivity sucks, it best be in my jacket pocket and even there not always reliable.
Sound quality - OK, nothing fancy, but I am not an audiophile so it's good enough for me.
Mic quality - sucks, I got a lot of complaints from the other call party that they can hardly hear it or I sound gargled.

Oh, well. Third set will be returned tomorrow.

I wish there was another manufacturer who would follow this form factor and bean buds idea, but could not find any. Do you know of any?

The form factor wins thee day for me - the idea of putting a bean in your ear - is just brilliant. These are the most comfortable buds I ever had (and I tried quite a few) - even in the most intense workout they just keep in my ears. They don't "stick out" in any way. Love it!

Why do I return them?
The Bluetooth connectivity is just horrible. And I am a Samsung person - Samsung phone, Samsung tablet, Samsung watch - so I thought what can get wrong with Samsung buds? I made sure I am on the latest firmware update on every buds set.
Apparently I was wrong: echoing between the buds, delays between the buds (one bud is a bit delayed after the other), losing connectivity to the buds, losing connectivity to one bud - all at random.
Even if I place the phone in my pants pocket - connectivity sucks, it best be in my jacket pocket and even there not always reliable.
Sound quality - OK, nothing fancy, but I am not an audiophile so it's good enough for me.
Mic quality - sucks, I got a lot of complaints from the other call party that they can hardly hear it or I sound gargled.

Oh, well. Third set will be returned tomorrow.

I wish there was another manufacturer who would follow this form factor and bean buds idea, but could not find any. Do you know of any?

title: Some customization, few features that work, backlight is terrible!
text:
For a smart watch at this low price I expected better. The backlight is Terrible! Don't expect to see time on a sunny day. The customization is better than others in this price range but receiving messages looks like I was back in the 90's.

The watch is a ok buy if you want something more than just a digital watch. But it only has 1 preset for numbers when you want to personalize it with a backscreen image. And again in sunlight you will never see the time Ever! I had a cheaper watch that done better and was able to see it no matter how bright the sun was.
The sleep part of the watch doesn't work! Don't buy this if that's what you want to keep track of.
I honestly wish I would have spent a little extra money and got a samsung watch.

title: punches well above it's weight class (okay, only somewhat...)
text:
Update, 10/26: far less enthusiastic about the performance of the watch. (Dropped two more stars.) Randomly decides to turn its Bluetooth switch on, and answer calls, even when I've SPECIFICALLY turned it off so it can't. Then as of yesterday the watch started randomly rebooting itself every few minutes. I've done a factory reset, will reinitialize it one more time. Note: the watch functionality has been marginal to this point, both with an old Motorola Z3 Play running Android 9 and a new Samsung A53 5G running Android 12. And NOT functional enough to offset it's irritating quirks. Even though it checks a lot of boxes, it doesn't perform them well over time...

Update, 10/4: three weeks of use, I've now dinged the watch and/or app a star, based on the sleep function. Sunday morning, I took the watch off, put it on the bathroom counter, and wore a nicer one to church. Later that day, I checked the sleep quality from the night before. Said I'd slept 14 hours, as it was counting the time on the counter as "sleep".

Original: I've been using the watch a little over a week now. I have to admit to being pleasantly surprised at it's functionality, especially when considering it's price point. I had previously bought my son a Samsung Galaxy watch (Bluetooth) that is about 4x more expensive; this has far better bang for the buck in my opinion. Comparing with my son, I've yet to find something the Galaxy watch does that this one doesn't.

And while I'm very impressed with the physical watch, I am far less impressed with the accompanying app. It seems odd that during the app setup, you pick "Other" to configure the watch. The app is a bit cumbersome to install (at least on an older Moto Z3) and properly configure. (For me, the third time was the charm.)

I have been using a Amazfit Band 5 since January. And while I really like the G-Tide, the 5's app did track some items better. Walks, for example, are mapped in the interface on the 5; not so on the G-Tide. Sleep on the 5 was tracked in four stages (Awake, REM, Light, Deep) versus three in the G-Tide (Awake, Deep, Light). The G-Tide tracks "Mood" in terms of an Emotion Record: either it doesn't accurately track when I'm "explaining" to the roofing contractor, or the "explaining" doesn't bother me as much as I had suspected. The 5's alarm could be configured to vibrate only, but I've yet to be able to turn the sound off the G-Tide's alarm. The 5 does have a smaller footprint, but the G-Tide is far more legible. I don't think I'll ever get 10 days battery life out of it, that way I have it configured and fiddle with it, but it is using between 10-20% per day. Be SURE to configure the "Do Not Disturb" function. First night I ended up taking it off as it buzzed/beeped every few minutes...

The delineator comes in terms of the Bluetooth phone functions. Call quality is acceptable, and the ability to see the first couple lines of an inbound email lets you know if you need to pull out a phone or laptop.

title: So far, not up to Samsung standard
text:
My wife is handicapped, and I got this watch to call me if she falls. I have purchased all three generations of Samsung watches for myself, and I have been satisfied with battery life. My wife uses no apps on the watch, does not even check the time, and the battery will not last 24 hours.
I am now trying various settings to see if I can extend the battery life without affecting fall detection using either Bluetooth or Wi-Fi. If I cannot extend the battery life, it will go back to Amazon, and I will go to a more reliable product - whatever that may be.

title: Seriously poor product
text:
I was excited to read a good review on CNET. So I went for it. I don't want to leave long winded review. But here it goes...

Battery life seems to be very good. That's the only good thing about this device :(
I had somewhat moderate expectations based on the price. But I thought if battery is that amazing the rest could be tolerable if it functions more or less ok.
Unfortunately it is not. It is just a watch, very cheap looking, and surprisingly small. Other functions are useless.

I tried to use its sleep tracker since I am a programmer and go to bed very late, around 2 am.
The watch shows me that I go to sleep randomly at 9:30 PM, 11 PM, which is about 3 hours off.

I tried to use exercise tracker for my sessions on stationary bike. There is no such option so I used treadmill. I push myself pretty hard with pulse going higher than 150 bpm most time. But during my 45 minute session was stubbornly showing pulse at 65-75 bps. It only spiked to 146 for few minutes. Seriously? How stupid is this? Even better, it refuses to actually record it as a workout anyway when I try to stop the session.

Bluetooth loses connection to my phone when I step away from it more than 4 meters in my apartment. Even cheapest Bluetooth headsets do not do that to me. My Samsung watch never lost connection once either.

Alerts are tiny and repeat every time until you remove them after scrolling them all. Very hard to read and annoying.

I guess you get what you pay for :( Very disappointing product

title: Not bad for price. Too big for wrist!
text:
So right off the bat, it is too big. I think the wristband is adjustable but I have yet to figure out how to do it without damaging it. As it is now it is too big for me to wear. (My wrists are average size for an adult woman.)

Secondly, there are a handful of typos in the instruction book. Missing letters, misspelled words, etc. The instruction book contains 5-6 languages though. Also a little round plastic disc shape fell out of it when I opened it, with a “front” and “back” stickers on each face. Not sure what the purpose of this thing is, it is just slightly smaller than the watch face but it’s not adhesive, so no idea.

Anyway, I’m still messing with it. It is very pretty And even if it’s only usable as a watch and step counter I’d be happy with it. It’s not a bad price and I don’t need all the bells and whistles of say, an Apple or Samsung smart watch. For the price, it’s not bad. I just hope I can figure out how to adjust the strap so I can wear it.

title: If you are in the US, don't buy from Online King
text:
(model SM-R3600ZBAXAR)

The watch performed well for about a year, except for a really short battery life, measured in half days. A piece fell off on August 13th 2018 and now the watch doesn't read the heart rate anymore. The other functions work, but the heart rate monitor and compatibility with my Galaxy S7 was why I bought THIS watch.

August 13th, 2018 - Called Samsung US support (Randy), and he was so useless he couldn't even enter the device SN, and that it wasn't the right number
August 16th, 2018 - Called Samsung US support (Jolisa), and she was able to enter the same number and had me send it back for repair.
August 23rd, 2018 - Samsung (Dexter) called back and said they had to return the watch un-repaired because it was an international model and they didn't have parts or software to fix it. He directed me to call Samsung International support.
August 23rd, 2018 - Called Samsung International support (Keno), who said that because it was purchased in the US, that it has to be fixed in the US and then had the audacity to tell me to take it to Geek Squad, who of course said that because we didn't buy it at Best Buy with the protection plan, that we were out of luck.
September 9th, 2018 - The watch was FINALLY returned, still broken.
September 25th, 2018 -

Online King, are you going to support the product that you sold?

Amazon, are you listening? If vendors are selling products out of zones for support, without properly representing that the product is not domestic, sure sounds like fraud to me. What recourse do I have?

My 15 year old Timex still works and it cost far less than the Samsung Gear Fit 2.

title: What I like most about the device is that it syncs with ...
text:
This is a frustrating device, though apparently, I don't use it the way it's supposed to be used. Apparently, it's supposed to be used as an all-day watch, so it learns and recognizes your heart rate for various activities. I wanted a way to monitor my heart rate during exercise and I particularly wanted a device that measured calories burned during strength training. Polar used to make such a device, but no longer does and mine died, so I had to come up with something else. My brother likes the vivoactive HR and has compared it to a heart rate strap and says its very accurate.

That said, I don't want to wear it all day -- for starters, I work at home and don't need to put on a watch. I have a Samsung GearS2 that has phone capability, so I tend to wear that out. So apparently, according to Garmin support, not wearing the device all day is why my heart rate is inaccurate enough that it takes me 2 or 3 restarts of the watch to get it to recognize the heart beat correctly. But -- since I don't know of another device that measures calories burned during strength training, I guess I'm just going to have to get used to it or buy the strap.

What I like most about the device is that it syncs with myfitnesspal so I don't have to enter exercise and calories burned manually and it calculates the new total of calories available to me. Last night that failed to work; today, I called Garmin and they walked me thru signing out of the Garmin Connect phone app, force stopping it, clearing cache and data, restarting the phone and then repairing the device and everything was fine.

I will say Garmin support was helpful; just frustrated that I can't use it like I would a regular heart rate monitor without restarting it. Would I buy it again? Hmm. I would like to say no -- or even return the thing -- but half my daily exercise routine is strength training and the only way I can lose weight is to accurately count calories burned and calories consumed, so...

Polar used to make such a device, but no longer does and mine died, so I had to come up with something else. My brother likes the vivoactive HR and has compared it to a heart rate strap and says its very accurate.

That said, I don't want to wear it all day -- for starters, I work at home and don't need to put on a watch. I have a Samsung GearS2 that has phone capability, so I tend to wear that out. So apparently, according to Garmin support, not wearing the device all day is why my heart rate is inaccurate enough that it takes me 2 or 3 restarts of the watch to get it to recognize the heart beat correctly. But -- since I don't know of another device that measures calories burned during strength training, I guess I'm just going to have to get used to it or buy the strap.

What I like most about the device is that it syncs with myfitnesspal so I don't have to enter exercise and calories burned manually and it calculates the new total of calories available to me. Last night that failed to work; today, I called Garmin and they walked me thru signing out of the Garmin Connect phone app, force stopping it, clearing cache and data, restarting the phone and then repairing the device and everything was fine.

I will say Garmin support was helpful; just frustrated that I can't use it like I would a regular heart rate monitor without restarting it. Would I buy it again? Hmm. I would like to say no -- or even return the thing -- but half my daily exercise routine is strength training and the only way I can lose weight is to accurately count calories burned and calories consumed, so...

title: Dissapointing at best
text:
There's multiple reasons I was upset with this watch:
1) The ECG function says it works for Android, but what they don't tell you is that it is ONLY available for Galaxy phones. Not to mention it is limited regionally.
2) The blood pressure function is limited regionally.
3) Updates for this watch were trash. They never fix issues which the users complain about. A great example is Strava. This watch (which I bought primarily as a running and cycling watch) did NOT play nice with Strava and on the developers side it was over 6 months and the best that could be done was extra steps with a work around solution. Really sad to see. Older Samsung watches were getting better updates.
4) You pay a premium for this titanium watch and the coating flakes off rather easily with wear. Not to mention the bezel can be scratched pretty easily. VERY shoddy capability in that regard for the price.
5) The mobile app for this watch could be streamlined much better. Garmin is where it's at. I upgraded to a Garmin Fenix 6X Pro and got everything I needed.
6) It's heavy. For all titanium I expected strength but not weight. What the heck did they put inside this thing?
7) The rotating bezel on top is gimmicky at best and dings up easily.
8) The charging cable is a magnetic floating back. Easy to bump off and requires laying the watch face down with the cable slack and in a very precise position. Really stupid engineering design on that one.
9) Probably most important is the horrible battery life on this thing. At best I could get 2 days out of it without doing lots of exercise. It took a lot of planning to use this thing due to poor battery life requiring charging. If you have to charge it while you're asleep then the sleep tracking is kind of worthless right?

So, if you want a watch that looks snappy (until it gets easily scratched/dinged) and has a ton of flaws that honestly require immediate attention for the next models...then this is the watch for you. I was so dissapointed in the lack of thought put into this watch and/or it's upgrades that I'll probably never get a Samsung watch again. My Garmin watch doesn't have a single one of these flaws.

title: Battery Stinks. App needs to compete w/Fitbit
text:
BATTERY:
I paid more for the 44mm screen because I read the battery life was better and was disappointed. Be prepared to charge your battery every day. I thought I could handle this because I have a note 10 and can power share, but it's ridiculously slow to charge, and I'm unable to use my phone while it charges. It's not convenient.

Wristband:
The one that came with my watch was way too big. I had to pay extra for small bands. I'm the standard human being and couldn't be a more average size. 5'6" 145lbs

App:
I switched from Fitbit and can honestly say the app is way worse. Samsung had a huge opportunity for sales after Fitbit's last update failed and made a lot of their customers upset. The community page is international, which makes it pretty pointless to share your workouts. The weight management function is confusing and encourages me to burn calories before I have even eaten that a day. I don't really use the sleep function because I'm usually charging my watch during the night. I wish it had a calendar view of all of my workouts over the past month, so I can see if I'm staying on track. I also wish that their were more individual challenges for workouts and steps. The challenge for June is a run through the broccoli forest, which is a bit juvenile for my taste. Is Samsung's target market kids or adults?

That being said. I do like that I can answer phone calls, send texts with my voice, GPS my workouts, view the weather, and submerge it in water. The screen is super bright compared to my old fitbit, which is why it sucks the battery so quickly, but worth it for the ability to read it in the sunlight. The watch feels very James Bond-ish with it's new tech and high-resolution screen.

The watch is an eye-catcher, but I wish the functionality was better than Fitbit. I'm pretty disappointed, and would probably get a versa with the better App and battery life/faster charging after having used it for a month. Maybe there's a better app to pair with the Samsung watch, but I haven't found it yet. Hopefully Samsung has an update that better meets their customer's needs.

title: Battery life stinks
text:
So I upgraded from my fitbit versa 2 to the galaxy watch 4. I knew the battery life on the galaxy watch would be a huge downgrade from the fit bit which I only have to charge once a week but I was prepared to sacrifice that because the other features would balance it out. You will have to charge the watch daily.

The watch band is super soft and comfy. I love the interactive bitmoji watch face. I'm still figuring everything out and it took forever to figure out how to get Messenger notifications on the phone. Thank God for YouTube. At first I thought I couldn't use the apps I needed and was ready to return. I'm glad I did more research. All in all I love the look and feel of the watch and love the texting and calling feature. Samsung really needs to work on the battery life because I hate having to charge it everyday but I will learn to live with it for the upgraded features.

title: Mediocre
text:
Mediocre fitness band. GPS was nice, battery life was great...but no battery indicator if you don't use one of twelve watch faces...and the battery life is 4 or 5 menus deep. Very nice shape for a refurb, unfortunately mine had a weird smudge on the screen that didn't go away with isopropyl alcohol wipes, and I didn't want to use anything more aggressive. Not sure if it was under the screen or something else.

I ended up returning mine mainly because of the smudge, but I also had a hard time getting good HR measurements. I compared it wrist to wrist with my Samsung smartwatch, and one time it was very close, but another it was 50bpm off. I had to move it to the bottom of my wrist for it to pick up the correct signal, but it lost it again after putting it back on top. Very disappointed. I have very few problems with my Samsung watch, so I don't think it's skin color or position or anything like that...it's just not the greatest sensor.

title: From a runner's perspective
text:
I bought these headphones specifically for running outside. I had previously purchased in-ear headphones, but found they are quite bad for outside use because when the wind hits them they generate a lot of unwanted noise. I found in-ear headphones are really only good for indoor running. These have no issue with the wind, as the foam earcups resolve that issue. They also connect to my Samsung watch to play Spotify offline, so I can basically just put on these headphones and wear my watch and go for a jog, no need to bring my phone or anything else.

Pros:
- Lightweight, barely really feel them
- Wireless works very smoothly, even with my Samsung watch
- Foam earcups are removable for washing and come with an extra pair
- Amazing price point
- More comfortable than ear buds (at least for me)
- Can hear outside noises like cars etc. for safety

Cons:
- Sound quality will certainly not impress any audiophiles but fine for casual listening

Overall they fit a niche for me. Headphones that can be used for outdoor running that are over the ear, rather than earbuds. I don't know why more headphones don't exist in this category. I also have a child and plan to run with a running stroller. If I used earbuds I might not be able to hear a vehicle etc. These allow you to easily hear ambient noise. I run with them at a lower volume so I can hear basically everything but also enjoy my tunes. I'm really happy with my purchase so far. The only downside is the sound quality. If they had a higher quality sound version with the same build I'd definitely purchase them.

title: Accurate calorie burn! Incorporates heart rate into determining activity calorie burn
text:
I came to Garmin late. I had been a long time Fitbit user and when my Fitbit charge 2 crashed after 2 year's I decided I needed a change and an activity tracking watch that would last longer. I tried both the Fossil Juliana watch and the Samsung Galaxy watch in May 2020 before I tried Garmin. My problem with both the Fossil and Samsung watches Is that even though they both track heart rate, they do not actually incorporate your heart rate into calorie burn during activities. So if you were to take your watch off, Put it down on the table, and start an activity with either the Fossil and the Samsung watch they would give you the exact same calories burned as if you actually had your watch on your wrist and were walking, or if you had your watch on and were running hard. They both have a preset calorie burn per activity level which makes no sense. I think it's ridiculous that they do not incorporate their heart rate tracking into determining the activity calorie burn.

So finally I got to the Garmin venu watch last and I absolutely love it. It tracks heart rate and pulse ox, it incorporates heart rate and intensity level of exercise to give you a more accurate calorie burn, similar to what Fitbit does. I find that the calories burned on the Garmin tend to be a little bit under what I previously got with Fitbit, but Garmin is likely more accurate. I think Fitbit usually overstated calorie burn. I count my daily calorie intake and I find that with my Garmin watch when I have a net zero calorie deficit my weight stays the same, and when I have a calorie deficit i lose weight. So I find it very helpful for weight maintenance. If you have a Fitbit and are thinking of switching to a different brand definitely choose Garmin If you need to watch for activity and calorie tracking. I also like the GPS function for tracking outdoor runs and walks on the Garmin device.

The only thing that is terrible about the Garmin watch is the sleep tracking, it is completely inaccurate and overestimates sleep. Basically as long as you are laying down the watch is going to say you were sleeping and and it says that I get 8 hours of sleep every night, despite the fact that I struggle with insomnia. Fitbit was much more accurate at sleep tracking. The Garmin is also more expensive but I'm hoping it lasts longer than the Fitbit.

title: Worst Customer Service Ever
text: Let me tell you about  my Samsung active 2 watch journey – We bought the watch last fall  – in June the back slid off – checking the Internet this is a common problem – the watch was repaired under warranty and returned – a few days later it stopped working – we sent it back and they told us there would be a charge because we removed the back – we complained and they repaired it under warranty again – when we got it back it lasted only a few hours and stopped working – we sent it back again and now they want $219.79 to fix it since we damaged it and the warranty is now void. They claim the watch got wet but in the add "Finally, in terms of hardware, the Galaxy Watch Active features the same dual-core 1.15GHz chipset and 4GB of internal storage space as the Samsung Galaxy Watch. It's waterproof to 5ATM and rated at IP68, so you can use it to track your swimming sessions" so after the watch back was replaced it still leaked water. the customer service may be the worst I have ever experienced.  We have escalated the issue through our credit card company . Hopefully they can do something

title: Battery life far less than a day
text:
The battery life of this watch is the worst I've ever encountered. My Garmin Fenix HR3 lasts two or three days using all features, including heart tracking, exercise tracking. This Galaxy watch 4 classic lasts LESS THAN 8 HOURS with most features turned off
What is Samsung's recommendation to fix this? Turn off all networking and disable the health apps. In other words, turn this overpriced smart watch into a useless simple timepiece.
I'd recommend against purchasing.

title: An iPhone Review ....
text:
UPDATE 4-20-17
My watch died overnight while on the charger. Can't get it to come back on, reset or anything. It was great while it lasted.
Also, my wife lost hers because of the poorly designed clasp.
I'm starting to lose faith in Samsung products not just because of this product but others as well.
*****************************************************************************************************************

For all those who have been waiting for the iPhone app, the wait is now over......
I purchased my Gear Fit 2 new on the big E at a very reasonable price. I set mine up using my iPhone 6S. If you are currently set up using an Android device and you want to use it on iPhone, you must reset it and setup using the iPhone. Go to the Apple app store on your phone and download the free "Samsung Gear Fit" app. Make sure Bluetooth is enabled on the phone and make sure the watch is near by. Start the Gear Fit app and it will walk you through the set up process. Very easy and worked flawlessly for me. I understand that it might not be up to par with everything available for the Android phones but it is working out great for me and since I never had it paired to an Android phone, I don't know what I'm missing.

Battery life:
I don't wear mine to bed so the battery life is not an issue for me since I charge it every night.
I have it set up so the display is always on so that eats even more battery.
Still, at night when i put it on the charger I still have better than 50% left.

Heart Rate Accuracy:
This is the main reason I only granted 4 stars.
I compared the heart rate to my chest strap monitor for my Bowflex Treadclimber while I was working out and they do match once the Gear Fit 2 catches up to real time. It lags behind and does not seem to sample as often as my chest strap. I think this is pretty much par for the course on all of these wrist monitors.

Steps monitor:
Does a very decent job counting the steps and never seems to get confused between walking and riding in a car.
I like the fact that it allows me to set daily goals and alerts me when I obtain those goals. Does a good job counting the floors when I go up and down stairs.

Other nice features:
* email alerts pushed from the iPhone.
* Breaking news alerts pushed from various apps (Fox News, ABC news, etc)
* Vibrates when I have not been moving for 50 minutes and says "Time to Move'
* Automatically tries to pick up on what activity you are doing but I find it is best to manually tell it when you are about to exercise.
* Can play music directly from the watch or control iPhone
* Lots of customizable watch faces (analog is my favorite).

It sends data to the Samsung sHealth website to review your stats. (sHealth accounts are free).

One caveat is that it will not work with my iPad Air 2 which is no big deal for me since I can still go to the website to check on my stats.

The nice thing about this watch is the fact that the software will continue to emerge and get better over time but for now I am very satisfied and thankful to Samsung for this iPhone app.

title: newer version but not really an upgrade
text:
I bought two of these, one for myself and the other as a gift for my mother, I previously had the fit2 and really liked it, thought this would be an improved version and it is not really much of an upgrade, does not have features the fit2 had like music storage and bluetooth connection to headphones that I really appreciated (has a music control function, but needs to be connected to a phone, and I preferred not bringing my phone with me on runs with the fit2 I had) unfortunately I lost my fit2 and buying this makes me regret losing it even more...

the battery life is improved (but only on one of the two bands) my mothers barely lasts a day vs mine is 5+ days, so can see why some have issues since there seems to be problems with consistency in quality, some good some bad...? kinda seems like an afterthought product by samsung that they didnt put much effort into, also have issues with the charger, even though my battery lasts longer sometimes I have to wait until the watch completely dies before it will charge (at first I thought it was a defective charging base...which are ridiculously expensive *more than half the price for the watch to buy if you want an extra) but it seems to be the watch not recognizing it is on the charger...and not connecting (it is a wireless/contact charger)

it seems to do well with the basic functions, of counting steps, heart rate, etc and obviously it tells time, but not a lot of features other than that to make it worth the price (if it was priced around $50 it would be a good value, but not so great if you pay 2x that much) it syncs with my samsung phone well (would just get a fitbit if I didnt have the phone...) it gives updates from my phone (but need to be fairly close for it to connect so not really that useful since I am usually seeing the notifications on my phone the same time as seeing them on the watch) I like the size of the fit, not big or bulky and hardly notice I am wearing it, I have the Galaxy watch and it is too big so I dont wear it every day (switch between the two depending on what I am doing, on active days I am wearing this...)

it is not that user friendly and my mom has stopped wearing hers (battery life on hers was a lot lower so thats another big reason) she was just using it as a step counter and watch, and didnt need it to do much more, but wouldnt be able to use if for the other stuff because it isnt that easy to use, syncing it to the phone and all the stuff it can do is useful, but still takes opening apps on the phone and taking extra time to do it all so that is my main complaint on the user friendly part... need the samsung health app and the samsung wearables as well *an app that does everything would be nice instead of multiple ones... have a 3rd app for my samsung headphones and thats about enough that the compatibility reasons for why I used to buy samsung products might no longer be a deciding factor in my next tech purchases

I want to recommend this but really cant, I had hoped for so much more from Samsung and this is the letdown that has made me second guess my loyalty to the brand, so if you can get a really good deal on one, buy it, if not you can probably find a better deal on a fitbit (I have never owned a fitbit, but comparing my watch to friends and coworkers I wouldnt say I have the better watch)

title: Hot Garbage
text:
Shame on Samsung for luring in customers with beautiful hardware only to stick them with totally unfinished, nonfunctional software. I came to this watch from a Gear Fit 2 that I was very happy with over two years of ownership. I was mainly upgrading for the better design and the rotating bezel, figuring I'd pick up slightly improved battery life and a feature or two. Here's what I got, pairing Samsung's latest wearable to their latest phone (S8)

-Samsung Pay is completely nonfunctional on the watch, which is a bummer because it's basically the only added feature over my Fit 2. Holding the button to trigger it brings up the installation process on the phone, which fails, endlessly. Samsung Support was totally incapable addressing the problem.
- The watch randomly asks for a PIN to unlock the screen, even though it doesn't leave my wrist, and my security setting is set to "payments only" (which is ironic because payments dont work at all). Power cycling helps but only for a day or two, which is obnoxious because it totally breaks the "look down - get time - be happy" concept of a watch.
-Gesture-based screen waking is noticeably slower on the Gear Sport than the Fit 2, especially for notifications.
-Battery life without continuous heart monitoring is great during the day, burning around 25 percent during an active 16 hour day. Worn to bed, however, it burns roughly 50 percent tracking my sleep for 8 hours. Because...sure.
-Battery life suffers dearly when using continuous pulse monitoring
-Samsung has had ages to implement a chat history in Whatsapp, but still refuses to. So when my girlfriend attempts to communicate to me through haiku-stream-of-conciousness (as she constantly does) all I get is "...Pineapple"
-You would think with such a gorgeous OLED screen and several generations of round Samsung watches, that you would be able to find some really cool watch faces.I struggled to find three I liked and two had serious battery drain problems.
-Comparing the heart rate monitor on the S8 with the Gear Sport, the sport appears to consistently measure 10-20 bpm high, which my Gear Fit 2 from 18 months ago did not do. Progress.
-Connecting this watch to my S8 running the Oreo beta absolutely demolished my battery life. Less than 2 hours screen on time. Perhaps deliberate so that you're unable to post negative reviews or call Samsung Support.
-Bluetooth range was awful.Constant disconnections just walking one room away.
-It has a microphone yet not a single voice-oriented feature works. Not even talking incorrect dictation. It just says "voice app not available". The box should say "Features Mystery Hole to Nowhere".

Samsung has been building these watches for several years. There is absolutely no reason this thing should have made it to market in this state. It fails as both a smart watch (broken screen lock, no whatsapp) and a fitness device (poor HR accuracy and function, no external sensor support). My Garmin Vivoactive 3 wasn't as much fun to look at and fiddle with, and the software was far from perfect, but it still felt like a much more compelling solution for accurately tracking your health and fitness. In summary, the Samsung is the super attractive, but super crazy person you can't help but go on three dates with. Garmin is the slightly more wholesome one you take home to meet the folks.

title: You get what you pay for
text: So, out of the box this watch looks great. Unfortunately that's where it ends. The instructions were written by someone with a very basic understanding of English which made them hard to follow. Setup with the app is very difficult and didn't even sync with the watch at first. About a week after getting the watch, it started to fog up on the inside and had to be repeatedly put into a bag of rice to dry out. The sensor on the back became detached from the backing and started moving around, which made getting an accurate heart rate impossible. All in all, it was the cheaper option and I suppose I got what I paid for. I replaced it with a refurbished Samsung smart watch for about $100 more.

title: Doesn't work well for bicycling
text:
I bought this to keep track of fitness and my typical exercise is bicycling. On a 30 mile ride, it gives up after 12 miles one time and 14 miles another time, in the middle of the ride. It says something like "rest now, see you soom". Huh? Samsung health works best for bicycling, but didn't want to pay the crazy Samsung watch price for refereeing soccer.

As a watch, it's fine. Seems to monitor sleep time ok, but I don't really care about that.

I really would like a better countdown timer. I want it to remember my custom timers so I don't have to reset each time (like at soccer game half time).

Samsung Health let's me keep track of food and biking but short on other info. This watch fills the walking and refereeing need. I just have to add things myself for overall net calories for the day.

title: One of the worst watches to come from Samsung
text:
I had the Gear S3 when it came out in 2016. I bought this as an upgrade and wow do I regret it.

So many issues with this watch I don't even know where to begin.

First, the wristband that comes with the watch is so poorly made, it probably cost them 10 cents per unit.

You absolutely will need to buy a replacement band, the one that comes with the watch is SO CHEAP.

Additionally, there is no charger or cable provided with the watch. So as soon as you unbox it, the next thing you need to do is buy a charger.

For $500 you'd think Samsung could put a bit more effort into this. I will be returning it and never buying a Samsung watch again.

title: T-rex Pro activity tracker
text:
I bought the T-rex Pro because my Garmin rugged instinct stop tracking intensive minutes. Tracking my exercise activity for week is my main purpose for buy a fitness tracker. I was very disappointed to find out the T-rex Pro does not track your active minutes. What is does have is a PAI tracker, which is this devices intensive workout minutes tracker. The problem is that it will only track those minutes where your heart rate is at it's very highest for 10 minutes or longer. That is just crazy, so the only time you will get PAI point is when you sprint or do other high intensity cardio. My main workout are weight lifting followed by jogging, cross fit, and elliptical.

The other thing I thought the Garmin rugged instinct did better was notifications, when you get a text on the T-rex Pro you have to swipe over to the notification screen to see the text and it will only show text. On the Garmin instinct you just look at you watch and right there the notification will populate without have to touch or swipe the device and it will show text and Facebook messages.

Step and mile counter, I found it to be way off from what the Garmin rugged instinct, Samsung gears fit and the fitbit alt show. While on other devices it will show 15,000 to 16,000 steps 7.5 miles the T-rex Pro shows 10,000 steps 4.5 miles that is far less than the other devices.

I do not like the zepp app it is always running in the background and you are limited your goals selection.

The good points are it is very nice looking and has many different face watches to choose from. It will also let you pick you main activities on your workout selection, and It does have a wide verity of workout to choose from. You can manually calculate you workout minutes, but then you can do that on any device. The battery life last 5 day, I like the watch always on feature and I have a workout for every day. Those that say it last 18 day must only wear it as a watch with widgets turned off. In closing I am still on the hunt for the best device for my workouts.

Very little time to set up, very little directions needed and it was Simple and easy for a tech tard like me.
This will hold me over until I can get a new Samsung watch.

title: Not a Bad Watch 👍🏿👍🏿👍🏿 But Wait .... There's more.
text:
Wasn't sure what to expect. For some reason, I couldn't get it charge out of the box until I connected it to my Macbook USB. That took me a few days to figure out. After that first charge, I was able to charge it using a brick. Keeps a nice charge and some cool features. Only thing I miss (after having a Samsung watch first) is being able to tap an alert and have the respective app open on my phone.

UPDATE Oct 2020: Just stopped taking a charge. Won't come on. I've tried different bricks and plugs, etc. Been off for about 1.5 weeks. No signs of life. #booooo

UPDATE Jan 2021: TicWatch sent another charger and it seems to work fine again. Apparently, FAST CHARGER bricks (you likely have laying around esp of you have a Samsung phone) can kill the charger causing it to stop charging the phone. Seems like they would send their own bricks with their USB charge units. 🤔

title: My favorite Wear OS watch I've ever owned!
text:
Now let me start off real quick with saying that DHL was a big pain to deal with in regards to shipping. They somehow didn't get my correct address so it was stuck in Austin for 3-4 days even after I sent them an address correction. They'd say "It'll be delivered today", but it never came till days later. But on the bright side, it still did come way before was was originally projected as the delivery date which I think was October 13th. All of this just to say be sure to keep track of the shipping once you get your tracking number just in case there's an issue!

But other than that, the experience I've had with this watch has been the absolute best! The battery is way better than the original TicWatch Pro I had and even my Samsung Gear Active that I used to have as well! I've always loved Wear OS but the sluggishness that plagued the original Android Wear watches turned me away. But with updates and newer hardware, it's become soooo much better! I was pretty excited when I heard that the TicWatch Pro 3 was going to have a new and updated processor and it's been great! Like I said the battery life is stellar and I haven't noticed any lag at all. The new backlight added to the dual layer outdoor display is a great addition too, so now I can see it at night. Lastly, the Pro 3 is much lighter than the orignal TicWatch! It's really noticeable when you compare them side by side. Sleeping with it on hasn't been an issue because of how lightweight it is. Speaking of sleeping with it on, the sleep tracking app has worked well too. I use it with the Mobvoi app on my phone so I can see how good or bad my sleep patterns are. The watch even gives recommendations on how to get a better nights sleep based on your own sleep patterns which is nice. I've really enjoyed the TicWatch Pro 3 and I can't recommend it enough!

All of this just to say be sure to keep track of the shipping once you get your tracking number just in case there's an issue!

But other than that, the experience I've had with this watch has been the absolute best! The battery is way better than the original TicWatch Pro I had and even my Samsung Gear Active that I used to have as well! I've always loved Wear OS but the sluggishness that plagued the original Android Wear watches turned me away. But with updates and newer hardware, it's become soooo much better! I was pretty excited when I heard that the TicWatch Pro 3 was going to have a new and updated processor and it's been great! Like I said the battery life is stellar and I haven't noticed any lag at all. The new backlight added to the dual layer outdoor display is a great addition too, so now I can see it at night. Lastly, the Pro 3 is much lighter than the orignal TicWatch! It's really noticeable when you compare them side by side. Sleeping with it on hasn't been an issue because of how lightweight it is. Speaking of sleeping with it on, the sleep tracking app has worked well too. I use it with the Mobvoi app on my phone so I can see how good or bad my sleep patterns are. The watch even gives recommendations on how to get a better nights sleep based on your own sleep patterns which is nice. I've really enjoyed the TicWatch Pro 3 and I can't recommend it enough!

title: Too Many Issues
text:
UPDATE #2: So, my replacement watch has now began to overheat, twice within the last 24 hrs. I feel the heat on my wrist and the watch face says I'm only allowed to make emergency calls. After about 2 to 3 minutes the screen says "Cooled....You can now use your watch." I'm not sure what causes this. Both times I've been wearing the watch for a few hours and haven't been doing anything specific. The last time was early in the morning just waking up, no alarm used. I'll be calling customer service or the insurance plan carrier. Don't think I'll purchase another Samsung Watch after this one. I never had this much trouble with my Fitbit until about 2 years later. I've only had this (replacement) watch since July 10th and it's only August 28th and it's already giving me trouble. I'm only keeping it because it wasn't cheap!!

UPDATE #1: The replacement is much better!! Been wearing for about 4 days now. No issues with the alarms. Vibrating as it should. And no over heating! I only wish this watch was supported completely by the WW app so that all my data (sleep info) would sync but thats ok. I can enter this stuff in manually (which, I probably wont). My main usage for this watch is the alarm and exercise options and those function fine. Glad I made the purchase and there weren't any issues with replacing the original defective watch for a new one. Amazon customer service is THE BEST!!!

ORIGINAL REVIEW: I've only had this watch for about 2 weeks and I've had trouble since day 1 mainly due to the alarms. I've set them up the correct way (had 2 smart watches previously and alarms were used without an issue)!! Turn the Sound settings to Mute and the Vibrate to a certain vibrate setting. My issue was the watch not vibrating!!! The watch screen just lit up showing the alarm going off but no vibrating! I tried deleting the alarms, created new ones which didn't help. I lost sleep watching the clock all night in fear that I'd be late for work (good thing i did because the watch never vibrated). I then did software updates as advised which those took insanely long (about 2 hours). Eventually, the alarms seemed to work (but I never trusted it lol). I turned on a back up clock and phone alarm (which will wake my spouse), just in case the watch reverted back to its old habits. With the alarms now working, this morning the watch started to feel increasingly hot against my skin, thought I was just feeling things. But no, about 10 minutes later the screen said the watch needs to cool and the only function that will work is making emergency calls. I shut the watch down, waited a few minutes, turned it back on, and it did the same thing. I shut it down a second time, and the same thing happened!! With all these problems, i decided to just order a replacement because this most recent issue seems a little dangerous with this over heating problem. I sometimes leave my watch charging while in the shower and doing things around the house. I'm afraid of a fire beginning while I'm away due to it over heating. Or worse, I get burned because it suddenly starts heating up for no reason. Oh, and the battery life sucks! I've noticed it has something to do with the watch face, so i switched to a more simple, less active face and placed in Battery Saving Mode which resulted in some improvement however, the battery life is still pretty low at night after all day use and wont make 16-20 hours without being charged. I'll update this review once I receive the replacement watch. I really want it to work as it wasn't cheap, is super cute and i need a quiet vibrating alarm!!

title: Almost Didn't try them. So Glad I did!!
text:
A quick summary in case you don't want too much info. RUN to the store DON'T WALK and pick these up today!!!!

When I first received this unit I found that I did not like the color at all. Grey is the wrong shade for headphones like these. But forgetting about the color lets get into the whole device itself!!

The Good!

The headphones fit great! I love that they are not as uncomfortable as you would think they look. In case they current inserts are too small or too big there is 1 smaller and 1 larger size ear and lobe insert.

Battery life is amazing on the headsets for music or streaming I do wish it was a bit longer for phone calls.

Moving and pairing the unit between phone and PC is usually a hassle with Bluetooth devices but these moved between seamlessly without any issues. I LOVE THIS!!!!

The other thing that is amazing is that you can pass the 1 of the headphones to a friend, partner, Significant other, child, etc. and you can both listen in without worry of wires!!

USB-C charging so quick charge up with appropriate charging brick.

The headphones have some onboard memory as well so you can put songs on it for workouts or just a playlist to zone out to. It is less than 4 gigs but enough to get a good list of songs to listen to for a while. Nice surprise but not needed as I stream directly from my phone/PC.

Now the bad.

As I stated before I wish the grey was a different color grey more charcoal or just a darker shade in general.

You can only charge the headphones in the charging case. With wireless charging an option it should have been used instead,

As per charging there is no charging brick so you need to have one and make sure it is newer to otherwise it will be a slow charge.

Have to use Samsung Gear app for initial setup. I don't like being locked to software but at least it checks for and perform firmware updates.

Voice commands on the unit are not always heard and if sweating it will affect the touch controls. It causes them to react incorrectly not all the time but often enough to be a small annoyance.

Last but not least the sound quality is not amazing. Even though I say this I still think they sound great for the type of headphone they are.

So in closing:

Pros:

Fits well!
Battery Life - Music/streaming.
Paring and switching devices a breeze.
Share what you hear!
Onboard storage
USB-C Charging

Cons:

Bad color
Case only charging
No Charging brick
Must use app
Some touch issues if wet/sweating
Sound quality, not 100%

title: Good, not great (not worth $200 and upgrading from the original Vivoactive)
text:
Started with a Vivoactive, went to Samsung Galaxy Watch (returned due to lack of fitness ability), went to Vivoactive 3, returned and kept my original.

The Greats:
- SO comfortable! That band is so nice.
- Good fitness ability
- Intuitive side scroll and face scroll (Some on here wrote the side scroll was USELess and claimed to keep accidentally hitting it, they must have had some fat wrists....)
- Easy to use
- Customizable
- SUP/Kayaking mode is sweet

The Not so Greats:
- The HR monitor is not accurate - compared side by side to chest rate monitor and Vivoactive original - the Vivo 3 was about 20% off on Avg HR, and Max HR and 30-40% off on calorie burn - kind of useless
- Battery life is more like 3 days, not 5 (no GPS use)
- No music able to be downloaded onto it
- Display does not come on well with wrist movement

It's a GOOD fitness watch that tries to be smart (exactly how I described my original Vivoactive), but I am returning it as I just don't think the added benefits over my Vivoactive watch are there (24/7 HR monitoring - who cares! it kills battery / Some added workouts - eh / Music control - the new update on my Vivoactive now allows this / Paying with the watch - meh, I don't think that's that useful).

If this watch accurately recorded HR during a workout and got a SOLID 4-5 days of battery, I would keep it and retire my original Vivoactive ... but the price tag does not warrant it IMO.

text: Overall I like (not love) this watch. It keeps up with the common stuff (steps, stairs, heart rate etc) and has tons of applications I won't use. I got this as a replacement for Samsung watch that died in the water (supposed to be a swimming watch). The VA3 seems to be light in that area. Laps and open water basics only. No water Aerobics function. No HR when swimming. It does seem to be waterproof though.

title: Not a good investment
text:
Okay I bought my Vivoactive 3 six months ago and I think now I can give you a brief review on what I like and what I do not like :
what I like:
- it's a round watch, very light, you can sleep while wearing it without being annoyed at all
- The silent alarm works perfectly, and it wakes me up: me the very heavy sleeper.

What I do not like about it:
- it is smaller than it looks in the ad, if you are into small watches in general, or if you are a girl who prefers the colorful ones, then please ignore this point.
- everything in this watch is very much behind the current technology, in nearly everything! taking in consideration the unstable firmeware, which hardly can sync and keep the connection with the application on my phone. for example: right now i cannot maintain the connection between the app and the watch, even though my phone is connected to the watch via blue tooth, and I can get notifications from facebook and whatsapp!
- The screen is of a very low quality, and that blue hue that you hear about in other reviews is true! the screen is always bluish and blurry, and looks of a very old technology, very similar to my 6600 Nokia phone, also it is not responsive to the touch while at the gym. Another thing about the screen , when showering it doesn't differentiate between water drops and your figures, so you will have to lock it before showering, otherwise you might find your self calling your grandma without noticing.
- The applications on their appstore are nearly not there, and if you find one that yo u like - which is seldom- it won't work properly.
- very very small storage, I couldn't find enough of it to keep my 7 watch faces that i liked.

last but not least, if you are comparing this watch with the rest of the watches in the market, this is a very old technology watch, with a lot of issues and problems. My opinion: avoid buying it and consider Apple watch 2/3 or samsung Gear Sport. With Samsung and Apple you will find the apps, the storage, the quality and the firmware are much advanced than Garmin VA 3.

title: The screen and construction let this watch down.
text:
I returned mine for 2 reasons:
1. The screen is dull and dim. The colors are muted, and even on the brightest setting it's just not a great watch face. If you're expecting Apple, Samsung, or Motorola screen brightness, vividness, and sharpness you'll be sorely disappointed.
2. The watch feels plastic (it is) and cheap.
I purchased a 3rd gen. Moto 360, and I'm very happy. I've had Apple watches and Galaxy watches, and the Moto 360 is the best value by far, and it's gorgeous.
